title: Check Your Temperature
summary: Use a thermometer to check body temperature correctly.
learnerPromise: You will be able to use a digital thermometer to check your body temperature and understand what the reading means.
whyItMatters: Temperature is one of the first things checked when someone feels unwell. Knowing how to measure it correctly helps you decide whether rest, medicine, or a doctor visit is the next step.
realLifeUses: Checking if a fever means you should stay home from work or school
Monitoring temperature during a cold or flu to track if you are getting better
Sharing an accurate temperature reading with a nurse or doctor
Helping a family member or roommate check if they have a fever
materialsNeeded: Digital thermometer (oral, ear, forehead, or underarm type)
result: An accurate temperature reading and a basic understanding of what ranges suggest a fever.
youWillLearn: Choose the right type of thermometer for your situation
Follow the correct steps for oral, forehead, ear, or underarm measurement
Read and record the temperature accurately
Know when a temperature reading suggests calling a medical professional
miniChallenge: Find a thermometer in your home, read its instructions, and practice taking your own temperature. Write down the reading and the time.
steps: Wash your hands and check that the thermometer is clean and working.
Read the instructions for your specific thermometer type.
Position the thermometer correctly in your mouth, ear, underarm, or forehead as directed.
Wait for the thermometer to signal it is finished, then read the display.
Write down the temperature, time, and how you were feeling.
completionCriteria: You successfully take your temperature and record the reading
You can name the general temperature range that is considered a fever for your measurement method
You can list one or two times when a temperature reading means you should call a doctor
commonProblems: Eating or drinking right before an oral reading can affect accuracy
Not waiting long enough between readings can give different results
Different thermometer types can give slightly different normal ranges
tips: Wait at least 15 minutes after eating or drinking before taking an oral temperature
Use the same type of thermometer and same body location for consistent tracking
Clean the thermometer with soap and water or rubbing alcohol after each use
helpBoundary: Contact a medical professional if your temperature is very high, lasts more than a couple of days, or comes with severe symptoms like confusion, stiff neck, or trouble breathing.
